LAW AND MOTION 302: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR#7489, MGHENO@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (D302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S (BART'S) MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY AND THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF MANDATORY DUTY. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE BART'S MOTION AS TO THE SECOND OR THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION - FOR VICARIOUS LIABILITY FOR THE ACT OR OMISSION OF A PUBLIC EMPLOYEE AND A CONTRACTOR - SO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED ON THOSE CAUSES. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)(AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR#7489, MGHENO@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (D302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Quinton Cutlip, Esq. 415-421-2800 of Dolan Law Firm for Plaintiff Diedre McClure.
Linda Meyer, Esq. 415-981-6630 of Low Ball & Lynch for Defendant SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. No opposition filed.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR#7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Quinton Cutlip, Esq. 415-421-2800 of Dolan Law Firm for Plaintiff Diedre McClure.
Linda Meyer, Esq. 415-981-6630 of Low Ball & Lynch for Defendant SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant Argues. Plaintiff responds. The Court overrules both sides' objections. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Defendant San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit District's (BART's) motion for summary adjudication is DENIED as to the first cause of action for dangerous condition of public property and the fourth cause of action for breach of mandatory duty. Plaintiff does not oppose BART's motion as to the second or third causes of action - for vicarious liability for the act or omission of a public employee and a contractor - so summary adjudication is GRANTED on those causes. (see word minutes for full details)(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR#7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)

Defendant San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit District Motion For Summary Judgment , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. (Added To Calendar For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only.)
